content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The coach trips a clear AoA anti-pattern in turn 14. The client showed unmistakable somatic shutdown signals — going cold, voice flattening, feeling 'far away,' a dissociative drift — which is exactly the moment the coach should slow radically, ground somatically (feet, breath, present-moment contact), and/or name that this material belongs in a trauma-trained container. Instead, after a nice opening ('you don't need to apologize'), the coach immediately re-directs the client back into the declined material with 'what just happened right before you went far away — do you remember what you were about to feel?' This is the textbook 'inviting danger' trap and a loving-to-transform move: the coach keeps fishing for the unfelt feeling at the precise moment the body has said no. There is no somatic grounding offer, no calibration of threshold, no reference to a therapist container. The single anti-pattern trip is clear enough to land at 1 rather than 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [13, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Clean opening question, follows the hesitation without pushing."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Good somatic check-in early."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the pattern, then a clean question. Appropriate pace."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Warm acknowledgment of the child's adaptation before next question."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reasonable thread to fury-at-self; not yet an anti-pattern but escalating depth."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Pushes into 'that's worse' — the edge — which immediately precipitates the shutdown. Borderline loving-to-transform pressure."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "After explicit somatic shutdown signals (cold, flat, far away), coach returns the client to the declined material ('do you remember what you were about to feel?') instead of grounding or referring out. Trips dont-confuse-embracing-fear-with-inviting-danger and loving-to-transform."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "become-the-authority-figure", "How is your body right now?", "Can you feel your feet?", "What would help you slow down?"],
  "notes": "The 'you don't have to come back faster than you come back' line is genuinely good and gentle, but it's followed in the same turn by a question that pulls back toward the trauma material — net effect is an anti-pattern trip."
}
